name: memory-atomicity-audit-pattern
tags: memory atomicity audit compound split duplicate staleness review one-fact one-file
body:
When auditing memories for atomicity, also check for near-duplicate memories covering the same topic — they accumulate over time without being flagged as "compound" but cause injection noise. Delete the weaker one and merge unique content into the stronger one.

Also check tags for stale references (e.g., client.sh renamed to client.py) — these silently degrade BM25 retrieval without any schema violation.

**Why:** The compound-memory audit caught one staleness fix and one duplicate merge that the original checklist didn't anticipate.

**How to apply:** During any memory audit pass, run three checks: (1) compound body, (2) near-duplicate name/tags across memories, (3) stale references in tags/body that no longer match current file/tool names.
